title: Harness drift detection in self_evolve
description: Add a harness_drift_check() step inside the self_evolve pipeline that reads _DAILY_TASK_CONTRACTS (the declared harness spec) and compares declared time windows against actual task execution timestamps from recent logs. Flag any task that consistently executes outside its declared window — this is the operational definition of spec-runtime drift for Mira's harness.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: harness and model are always confounded in evaluation because the harness is invisible. For Mira, _DAILY_TASK_CONTRACTS IS the harness spec — but it's never validated against runtime behavior. As self_evolve modifies Mira's code over time, the declared windows can silently drift from what the scheduler actually does (e.g., a should_* function gets patched but _DAILY_TASK_CONTRACTS is not updated). The note's harder question — 'who ensures spec and runtime don't drift?' — is directly answerable here: the self_evolve agent itself should check this on each run.
diff_description: In self_evolve.py, add a function harness_drift_check() that: (1) reads _DAILY_TASK_CONTRACTS from core.py (import or parse the dict); (2) scans ~/Sandbox/Mira/logs/ for the last 7 days of task dispatch records, extracting actual start times per task key; (3) for each task, checks whether >20% of executions fall outside the declared (start_hour, end_hour) window; (4) if drift detected, appends a warning to the self_evolve output report with the task name, declared window, and observed median execution hour. Call harness_drift_check() at the end of the self_evolve run, after other checks. No existing behavior is changed — this is additive logging only.
